texto: There are things that money can't buy, things like fresh youth to replace the one you've hardly been aware of, things like lost opportunities which might conceivably have led to nothing, but which on the other hand might have led to fulfillment and serenity and new lives and passionate involvement. (Along, of course, with disinheritance!) And human nature being what it is this is the version you'll unquestionably believe.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Stephen Benatar: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de sentido da existência e experiência vivida. O trecho “There are things that money can't buy, things like fresh youth to replace the one you've hardly b…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que negocios, money, regret pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de regret e youth.
